description:
Missoula fact-check findings to fix:

1. : MCPS structure is wrong. The page says the district has only two middle schools (Washington and Meadow Hill) and frames Big Sky High as being in the surrounding area. Official MCPS sources show C.S. Porter Middle School is also an MCPS middle school, and Big Sky High School is an MCPS grades 9-12 high school in Missoula.

2. : The Missoula Public Library opening year is wrong. The page says the new building opened in 2022, but the new library opened/celebrated its grand opening in 2021.

3. : The Bob Marshall Wilderness proximity claim is overstated. Saying the complex is reachable within about an hour's drive from Missoula appears too aggressive; official Lolo National Forest access information places the Seeley Lake access area about 60 miles northeast of Missoula, with wilderness trailheads farther beyond that.

4. : Missoula hiking metadata is stale. The Missoula hiking meta description says , while the page body and current 30-mile content use .

5. : Missoula fishing metadata is stale. The Missoula fishing meta description says , while the page body and FAQ use .

Evidence checked during review: official MCPS school pages for Big Sky High and C.S. Porter Middle School, official UM enrollment pages, official Destination Missoula fishing page, official/authoritative Missoula Public Library opening coverage, and Lolo National Forest access information.
title: Fact-check Missoula pages
